nodes.context: New York street gang active in early 1900s, involved in extortion and labor slugging, based on Lower West Side.
Longtime New York street gang that controlled Lower West Side territory before being succeeded by the Marginals.
New York street gang; members were apprehended with Marginals in 1910.
New York street gang; members were apprehended with Marginals in 1910.
Rival gang driven out by the Marginals.

edges.evidence_quote: succeeded the longtime Hudson Dusters in their territory of New York's Lower West Side
edges.context: Marginals succeeded Hudson Dusters in territory, indicating a structural succession.

edges.evidence_quote: Smith was witnessed pursuing a member of the Hudson Dusters with a revolver in his hand
edges.context: Marginals member pursued a Hudson Dusters member with a gun, showing conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: After driving out the rival Hudson Dusters and the Pearl Buttons
edges.context: Marginals drove out Pearl Buttons, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: eight members of the Tanner Smiths were apprehended along with members of the Gas House and Car Barn Gangs in September 1910
edges.context: Members of Marginals and Gas House Gang were arrested together, suggesting possible cooperation or shared activity.
